messages.author: Mimas
messages.text: Hi @Dave (msi) when I started MeshCore, I was like you, there was no one on in Northern Ireland. As it turns out, I wasn’t the first. But others that tried it had given up. I figured I’d need to put a few repeaters up as “jumping on” points for others. So I put a few solar nodes up to see what would happen. It didn’t take long for others to find them, and start adding their own repeaters. Repeaters near the top of hills are really effective. Our link to the UK is hill to hill. About 100km. And it’s pretty solid. The repeaters on both sides aren’t anything special, just little whip antennas. If there’s any way you could reach a big town like limerick with, say 2 solar repeaters, I reckon the whole thing will take off. I highly recommend the Seeed Studio P1 Pro, which comes ready to go. (edited)

messages.author: Mimas
messages.text: @Dave (msi) When I started off, I thought at least I'd get some fun with the kids. I put a few repeaters up on high places, and somewhere along the way I realised I wasn't the first. @soup0r already had a repeater at his house that I discovered a few weeks after I started. What I didn't know was that he'd put a repeater on a nearby hilltop that had died. Despite seeing his repeater, a few weeks went by before @soup0r realised that I was there, and we started chatting. About a week later @FunkyDysfunction appeared, and then the networks slowly started expanding. At some point we put a couple of messages out on Meshtastic to let people know what we were doing. Of course if you put a repeater out over a big population centre you're likely to find people more quickly. We've definitely had people just appear on the mesh without any word of mouth. I'm an example. I found this stuff via YouTube, it was relatively inexpensive so I gave it a go. Of course a good proportion of people will try it and if there's no one to talk to, they just give up. So I think it's worth getting a repeater up soon! That's my 2 cents! (edited)

messages.author: Dave (msi)
messages.text: I'm surprised that you are talking about repeaters that can't get enough solar charge over the winter. I thought I was in the same boat with my Seeed Studio SenseCAP as it was losing about 2% charge each day just before Christmas. I put it down to short, cloudy days and near-freezing temperatures. It got down to about 30% charge before I realised that when I installed it I paid no attention to which direction it was facing. As it happened it was pointing north. Once I swung it round to be south-facing it fully charged within three days. Is it a case of shadowing from trees that is preventing your 'hibernating' repeaters from getting enough sun?

messages.author: Dave (msi)
messages.text: I spent some time today looking into sensors for MeshCore. My understanding is that Meshtastic has good support for sensors in that the firmware will detect sensors connected to the I2C bus and automatically send flood messages out every five minutes. However, this didn't sit well with the MeshCore designers due to the amount of traffic created. Therefore, although I believe MeshCore repeaters may well be able to detect the presence of a sensor connected to the I2C bus and take readings from the sensor(s), the values can only be read as telemetry values by logging into the repeater. That said, could a device be programmed to do the same and upload the results to a website?

messages.author: Bumblebee
messages.text: I just turned on a rak based nrf52 node and all I get is a solid red light, it dimms one time and nothing, no blue light flashing at all, and I can not connect to it via my phone, it looks like there is something critical wrong, any pointers, it was insed in a dry attic in a sealed plastic box with a small 2mm hole in the bottom to let out any moisture, it does not seem damp, but who knows what it was like before today. There is a very small screech / squelch sound when I plug it in, I do not recall that before, like a capacitor leaking, if there is such a thing. (edited)

messages.author: McDaWisel
messages.text: Hello everyone, I’ve just myself up with a cheap heltec v3 and a basic Amazon antenna in my attic in Dublin 14. Overnight I completely filled the memory of the device. I wasn’t expecting to hit anything, but I woke up to 350 contacts and a 50 chat messages (which I believe is the maximum the device can store). So now I’m interested in doing this properly. I’m in Dublin 14, my attic is about 65m above sealevel and from the attic have a good view across everything north of me, so that’s most of Dublin. Pretty good view to the south as well, up to the Dublin mountains. I haven’t set the device up as a repeater yet, I have a second one coming and a better antenna to do this properly, but would love any guidance this group can give! (edited)
